page: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance belonging to the Dalv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz. The system is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is premium storage capable for external block storage attachments.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing also demonstrate high efficiency. However, multi-core performance and memory subsystem metrics show notable limitations. The Passmark memory score falls into the bottom 10%, and memory bandwidth read-only tests for both small and large blocks are weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation tasks also show reduced relative performance due to these memory constraints.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4als_v6 offers a budget-friendly entry point for compute-focused workloads that do not demand large memory footprints or high memory bandwidth. The low memory allocation per vCPU represents a clear tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM inference. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, text processing, and basic utility workloads where raw single-core CPU speed is prioritized over memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine in the Dalv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of memory. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium external storage. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Conversely, the memory subsystem presents a clear bottleneck, with Passmark memory scores in the bottom 10% and weak read-only memory bandwidth. This low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core makes the instance highly cost-efficient for compute-centric workloads with minimal memory requirements. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, and text processing tasks rather than memory-intensive databases or complex multi-threaded compilations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is a 4-vCPU virtual machine featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC processor and 8.0 GB of RAM, offering strong single-core performance alongside a low-memory footprint for compute-focused workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 features 4 vCPUs, a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, and 8.0 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for low-memory workloads.
tagline: A low-memory, compute-focused virtual machine delivering strong single-core AMD EPYC performance for lightweight web serving and single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, representing a low 2.0 GB per core ratio
Delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Lacks local storage and GPU, relying on premium external storage
Exhibits weak memory bandwidth and low multi-threaded scaling
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, and utility workloads
